explanatory_strength.rationale: Round-two audit confirms a specific croupier mechanism: base fees on ¥1.34tn AUM, success-fee cyclicality (~65% of combined fees in FY2026), mid-cycle owner cash ¥137.14/sh versus peak ¥317.33, and explicit performance_fee_cycle_reserve. Competitive-advantage case remains partial: single GAAP segment, global ETF sponsors compete on product, and etf_product_pipeline ¥100/sh is bounded judgment without incremental ROIC proof. The Yahoo ¥757 price exactly matching BVPS ¥757.01 undermines the mispricing narrative unless a real trade confirms it.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Contract backfill closes round-one proof gaps: four additive components with 100% valid calculation proofs, component_sum_reconciles and no_double_counting both true, filing_facts_2026-07-24 reconciles finfo2026 and Pnotice2026-1. Material Hohn gaps persist: no targeted research response resolved live TSE quote or depth; yuho/EDINET segment economics absent; management_facts are six unverified boilerplate excerpts; price today conflicts between illiquid ¥464 last print (100 shares) and ¥757 Yahoo feed that may be mislabeled book value.

downside_control.rationale: performance_fee_cycle_reserve (−¥140/sh base) quantifies cyclical tail and net_financial_claims adds ¥65/sh book cross-check, but downside is not execution-controlled. Public feeds show two volume days in twelve months, no dividend floor, owner cash is market-beta with FY2026 peak earnings, and normalized step-down toward FY2025 levels is plausible without structural protection. Contract low case annualized return at ¥757 is 6.49%, below a typical hurdle if the feed price is real.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Contract base annualized return at ¥757 is 16.94% (low 6.49%, high 26.83%) on ¥2,264/sh additive value versus spot, marginally clearing a ~15% hurdle on paper only. Lawrence synthesis 38.04% and deep-dive headline 53.33% use parallel paths not audited as component proof. Realized return versus alternatives is unknowable until broker-verified executable price exists; at ¥464 last print implied return would be extreme but the tape cannot fill size.

claims.claim: Universal contract sums four additive components to ¥2,264.38/sh base (core_fee_engine ¥2,239, etf_product_pipeline ¥100, net_financial_claims ¥65, performance_fee_cycle_reserve −¥140) with 100% valid calculation proofs and no overlap double-count per evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-24.

FY2026 success fees were ¥14.316B (+53.6% YoY) versus base fees ¥7.869B (+17.1%) on AUM ¥1.3357tn (+2.9%), so performance fees were ~65% of combined fees while valuation anchors on FY2025 mid-cycle owner cash ¥137.14/sh, not peak FY2026 ¥317.33/sh.

Price input is unresolved: Yahoo ¥757 (2026-07-23) equals filing BVPS ¥757.01, while the last documented TSE trade was ¥464 for 100 shares (Jan 2026); contract returns at ¥757 are not an investable entry without broker confirmation.

strongest_counter_explanation: The market is rationally ignoring an illiquid micro-float: even proof-complete filing economics and a deep discount to mid-cycle owner cash cannot be harvested because there is no continuous price or liquidity, FY2026 earnings power is inflated by cyclical success fees that will mean-revert, and the ¥757 feed may be accounting book mislabeled as a market quote rather than a tradable mispricing.
most_important_missing_fact: Broker- or exchange-verified live TSE quote, bid-ask depth, and average daily volume post 1-for-20 split, to test whether ¥757 (Yahoo 2026-07-23, coincident with BVPS ¥757.01) or ¥464 (last 100-share print) reflects an executable entry price.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with combined base+success fees falling more than 25% YoY while Japan equity AUM is flat, without offsetting base-fee rate increases
Operating margin compresses below 35% on revenue while success-fee share stays above 50%, indicating fee compression rather than pure market cyclicality
Confirmed average daily volume remains below ¥50mn notional for six months, proving the discount is a permanent liquidity trap rather than a mispriced franchise
specialist_findings: Round-two competitive-advantage audit: contract backfill upgraded valuation_contract to decision_grade with valid proofs on all four components; overlap keys are clean. Hohn concerns partially addressed (proof completeness, filing reconciliation) but not resolved (live quote, yuho segments, management fieldwork). Audit flags: (1) core_fee_engine base ¥2,239/sh uses judgment growth 5%/3.5% and 16× exit aligned with Lawrence scenarios while low case uses 2%/12×; (2) etf_product_pipeline ¥100/sh is pure judgment with AUM/revenue facts attached but no incremental launch ROIC ledger; (3) net_financial_claims claim_share 8.59% of BVPS is holdco pass-through judgment; (4) Yahoo price ¥757 = BVPS ¥757.01 is suspicious and breaks reproducible entry economics; (5) no targeted research_response.json closed round-one missing-fact requests. Hohn power zone fit remains valid (croupier, financial_services). Vote unchanged watch pending executable price.
